topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into a quiet afternoon. It was from Mark. My stomach clenched, a familiar, tight knot forming beneath my ribs. I hadn't thought about him in years, not since the relentless teasing in the seventh grade had faded into distant, embarrassing memories. Hesitantly, I tapped the request. Seeing his profile picture—that same sneering grin I used to dread—made my breath catch. I scrolled through his recent posts, a strange mix of apprehension and a hesitant curiosity swirling inside me. I didn't immediately accept. Instead, I spent a good ten minutes just staring at the screen, my fingers hovering over the 'decline' button, a quiet sense of protective reserve settling over my shoulders.

[story 2]

When the ping arrived, I nearly dropped my coffee mug. Mark. The name alone sent a shiver down my spine, a cold prickle across my arms. I reread the request three times, my heart beginning a frantic drumbeat against my ribs. I felt a strange pull, an almost magnetic draw toward the past, even though I desperately wanted to retreat. I took a deep, steadying breath, smoothing down my shirt. I tapped 'accept.' A wave of awkwardness washed over me, but beneath it, a quiet resolve started to bloom. I decided I would observe, keep my distance, and see what this unexpected digital tether would reveal.

[story 3]

The alert flashed across my phone. It was him. The memory of his sharp words, the way he used to make everyone else shrink away, flashed through my mind, bringing a familiar flush of unease. I stared at the notification, my jaw tightening. For a long moment, I felt a profound sense of internal conflict—a war between the urge to shut it down and a surprising, gentle curiosity about where he was now. Slowly, I navigated to his profile. I didn't immediately respond. I simply lingered, absorbing the digital residue of a shared history, a soft warmth spreading through my chest as I considered the unexpected invitation. Then, with a deliberate movement, I hit the confirmation button.
